etymology.description: semantic 广 ("house built to depend on a cliff") + phonetic 占 -> shop
etymology.interpretation: The character 店 (diàn, "shop") combines the semantic element 广, representing a building or structure, with the phonetic element 占, providing the sound. The idea is of a structure where goods are stationed, forming a place to sell or trade items, hence a 'shop'.

parts.english: house built to depend on a cliff

examples.chinese: 我在书店买了一本书(我在書店買了一本書)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài shūdiàn mǎi le yī běn shū.
examples.english: I bought a book at the bookstore.

examples.chinese: 这个小村庄只有一家小杂货店(這個小村莊只有一家小雜貨店)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xiǎo cūnzhuāng zhǐ yǒu yī jiā xiǎo záhuòdiàn.
examples.english: This small village has only one small grocery shop.

examples.chinese: 每当我路过那家甜品店(每當我路過那家甜品店)，我都会停下来买些东西(我都會停下來買些東西)。
examples.pinyin: Měidāng wǒ lùguò nà jiā tiánpǐndiàn, wǒ dūhuì tíng xiàlái mǎi xiē dōngxi.
examples.english: Whenever I pass by that dessert shop, I always stop to buy something.
